verification_questions.question: Does the live sub now carry a `since` filter covering the Phase-1 fetch start timestamp, ensuring no event published during the gap is silently dropped?
Does the `processedIds` dedup set prevent double-processing of events that arrive in both the Phase-1 historical result set and the Phase-2 live sub (when `since` causes overlap)?
Do the existing unit tests pass with no regressions, and does the reproduction test (groups-member-profiles.spec.ts:175 fixme) become re-enableable?
Is the fix minimal — confined to `subscribeToGroupMessages` in `welcomeSubscription.ts` and any directly-needed helper, with no unrelated modifications?
Does the `since` filter correctly use Unix seconds (not milliseconds) to match the relay's timestamp format, and is the start timestamp captured before Phase-1 begins (not after)?
Does the fix handle the re-subscribe path correctly — when `subscribeToGroupMessages` is called again after a `groups` state change, does each new call establish its own `since` anchor independently?
Are there any other call sites of `subscribeToGroupMessages` or analogous fetch-then-live-sub patterns in the codebase that have the same gap and need the same fix?

deferred_followups: dm-history-eose-livesub-gap — same fetch-then-live anti-pattern in ContactChat.tsx:213-295 (excluded by contract non-goals)
cross-instance dedup on rapid re-subscribe — Codex round-0 medium; architectural, requires design discussion
